sound_type: thin whistle -- barely audible 'eee'. 2,000-6,000 Hz. Alarm and territorial. 2-10 m carry. E=2
ecosystem: Dense understory. Sword fern, salal, devil's club. Moist PNW forests. Burrow systems near streams.
family: Aplodontidae (FIRST -- most primitive living rodent family)

coordinates.description: Mountain Beaver in PNW moist forest understory. Burrow systems in soft soil near streams. Dense sword fern cover.

taxonomy_note.description: FIRST Aplodontidae. Most primitive living rodent. Sole surviving species. ~50 mya lineage. PNW endemic.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Aplodontidae: Mountain Beaver (206, E=2). Sole entry. Sole living species.
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Aplodontia from Greek 'haploos' (simple) + 'odous' (tooth) -- simple teeth. rufa for reddish-brown fur.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Aplodontia -- monotypic genus. A. rufa is the only living species in the family.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Rodentia. Most primitive living family in the entire order.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Aplodontidae: FIRST and likely ONLY entry. Sole living species.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Living fossil. Primitive kidneys, thermoregulation, skull. PNW endemic.

song_metrics.song_duration_s: Whistle: 0.1-0.3 sec. Brief. May repeat 2-3 times.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single thin whistle or squeak. Brief. Rare.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: 1-3 whistles in rapid succession. No extended vocalization.
song_metrics.timbre: Thin. High. No resonance. No carry. Barely detectable by human ear in field conditions.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=2. Barely audible. 2-10 m carry. Quietest mammal in engine. Acoustic invisibility.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Minimal. Species-wide consistency. All populations quiet.
song_metrics.key_property: THIN WHISTLE. 2,000-6,000 Hz. E=2. FIRST APLODONTIDAE. Most primitive rodent. PNW endemic. NOT a beaver. Living fossil ~50 mya. v1.49.347.

pelage.overall: Dark reddish-brown to brown. Dense, coarse fur. 0.8-1.5 kg. Stocky body. Vestigial tail (barely visible).
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Small, dark, stocky rodent with no visible tail. If seen at all, appears briefly at burrow entrance. Easily confused with muskrat at distance.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal. Year-round dark brown. Dense fur for moisture protection.

physical_description.overall: 0.8-1.5 kg (2-3 lbs). 30-45 cm body length. Vestigial tail (<3 cm). Dark reddish-brown. Stocky. Small eyes and ears.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Small, dark, tailless rodent at a burrow entrance amid sword fern. Cut fern fronds near burrow = Mountain Beaver occupancy.
physical_description.field_id: Almost never seen. Look for burrow entrances (round holes in soft soil, often 15+ per colony) and cut sword fern fronds drying at entrance. The animal itself is nocturnal and cryptic.

nodes.title: First Aplodontidae -- Most Primitive Living Rodent
nodes.description: ~50 million year lineage. Sole surviving species. Every other rodent family evolved after. Eocene origin. PNW endemic.

nodes.title: PNW Endemic -- Found Nowhere Else on Earth
nodes.description: Southern BC through WA/OR to northern CA only. Moist forest understory. The PNW is the last habitat for this ancient lineage.

nodes.title: E=2 -- Acoustic Invisibility
nodes.description: Thin whistle, 2-10 m carry. Quietest mammal in engine. Olfaction primary. Sound secondary. Strategy: don't be heard.

nodes.title: NOT a Beaver -- The Misnomer
nodes.description: No relation to Castoridae. 10-20x smaller. No dams, no lodges, vestigial tail. Settler assumption. Some prefer 'sewellel' (Chinook Jargon).

cross_references.relationship: E=6 to E=2. Maximum to minimum. Canyon CRACK to understory whisper. Opposite acoustic strategies.
Kid mew (E=3) to Mountain Beaver whistle (E=2). Energy continues dropping. The engine goes quieter.
Fawn (E=3, forest floor) and Mountain Beaver (E=2, burrow). Both quiet forest voices. Mountain Beaver even quieter.
ANCIENT LINEAGE! Blues rock (oldest genre, E=6) and Mountain Beaver (oldest rodent, E=2). Both ancient. Both PNW. +4 gap.
